questions.question: If a³ -a² ≤ a² + a ≤ a⁴, then what is the value of a?
questions.explanation: For a =0 0 ≤ 0≤0 So above given inequality is satisfied so a can be 0. For a =2 (2)³- (2)² ≤ (2)² + 2 ≤ (2)⁴ So above given inequality is satisfied so a can be 2. For a =-2 (-2)³- (-2)² ≤ (-2)² + (-2) ≤ (-2)⁴ So above given inequality is satisfied so a can be -2.

questions.question: If x² - 5 = 1/x², then what is the value of (8x⁶ - 8/x⁶)/32?
questions.explanation: x² - 5 = 1/x² => x² - 1/x² = 5 ----- (i) Cubing both sides, we get => (x² - 1/x²)³ = 5³ => (x²)³ - (1/x²)³ - 3 (x²)²/x² + 3x²/(x²)² = 125 ------ [(a - b)³ = a³ - b³ - 3a²b + 3ab²] => x⁶ - 1/x⁶ - 3x⁴/x² + 3x²/x⁴ = 125 => x⁶ - 1/x⁶ - 3x² + 3/x² = 125 => x⁶ - 1/x⁶ - 3(x² - 1/x²) = 125 => x⁶ - 1/x⁶ - 3x5 = 125 [Putting the value of equation (i)] => x⁶ - 1/x⁶ - 15 = 125 => x⁶ - 1/x⁶ = 125 + 15 => x⁶ - 1/x⁶ = 140 ------- (ii) Now, (8x⁶ - 8/x⁶)/32 = 8/32 x (x⁶ - 1/x⁶) = ¼ x (x⁶ - 1/x⁶) = ¼ x 140 [Putting the value of equation(ii)] = 35

questions.question: What is the value of 'm' in the quadratic equation my² - 3m + 9 = 0 which has equal roots?
questions.explanation: Quadratic equation my² - 3m + 9 = 0 has equal roots. here coefficient of y = 0 Then, D = 0 - 4 x m x(-3m + 9) = 0 -3m + 9 = 0 m = 3 m cannot be zero, if m = 0 the equation my² - 3m + 9 = 0 becomes invalid.

questions.question: Number of hens in an animal shelter is 75% more than the number of cows. If the number of legs is 380 more than the number of heads, what is the number of cows in the shelter?
questions.explanation: Let the number of cows be 'x' Number of hens = (175/100)*x = 7x/4 Number of legs = (7x/4)*2 + 4x = 7x/2 + 4x Number of heads = x + 7x/4 So, 7x/2 + 4x - x - 7x/4 = 380 19x/4 = 380 x = 80

questions.question: From a point A, the angle of elevation of the top of a tower of height 20m is 60°. From a point B which on the opposite side of the tower, the angle of elevation of its top is 45°. What is the distance between A and B?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/rrbimg/88824/image001.jpg) Distance between A and foot of the tower=20/tan60=20/√3 m Distance between B and foot of the tower=20/tan45=20 m Hence, distance between A and B=20(1+1/√3) m

questions.question: Deepti buys mangoes at the rate of 18 kg for Rs 288 and sells them at 16 kg for Rs 272. How much mangoes Deepti should sell to obtain a profit of Rs 72?
questions.explanation: Cost price of 1 kg of mangoes =Rs 288/18 = Rs 16 Selling price of 1 kg of mangoes = Rs 272/16 = Rs 17 Hence profit obtained by Deepti by selling 1 kg of mango = Rs 1 Hence she should sell 72 kg mangoes in order to realize a profit of Rs 72.

questions.question: S₁ is a series of 6 consecutive integers which are multiple of 3 and whose average is 1.5. S₂ is another series of 6 consecutive positive multiples of 5 whose 1ˢᵗ number is equal to the 3ʳᵈ number of S₁, what is the sum of S₂?
What is average marks scored by all 5 persons in 8ᵗʰ standard?
questions.explanation: Total marks scored by all 5 persons in English = (0.8 +0.65 +0.72 +0.4 +0.54) x 500 = 3.11 x 500 Required average= (3.11 x 500)/5 = 311

questions.question: What is the percentage marks scored by E in 8ᵗʰ and 10ᵗʰ standard together?
questions.explanation: Marks scored by E in 8ᵗʰ standard= 0.54 x 500 = 270 Marks scored by E in 10ᵗʰ standard= 0.69 x 700 = 483 Required percentage = ((270 +483)/ (500 +700)) x 100% = 62.75%

questions.question: What is the difference between total marks scored by all 5 students in 10ᵗʰ standard and total marks scored by all 5 students in 9ᵗʰ standard?
questions.explanation: Total marks scored by all 5 students in 10ᵗʰ standard = (0.75 +0.7 +0.55 +0.5 +0.69) x 700 = 3.19 x 700 = 2233 Total marks scored by all 5 students in 9ᵗʰ standard = (0.6 +0.8 +0.64 +0.36 +0.6) x 600 = 3 x 600 = 1800 Required difference = 2233 -1800 = 433.

questions.question: What is the ratio of total marks scored by A in all 3 standards together to the total marks scored by D in all 3 standards together?
questions.explanation: Total marks scored by A in all 3 standards together = (0.8 x 500) +(0.6 x600) +(0.75 x 700) = 400 +360 + 525 = 1285 Total marks scored by D in all 3 standards together = (0.4 x 500) +(0. 36 x600) +(0.5 x 700) = 200 + 216 + 350= 766 Required ratio= 1285/766.

questions.question: The average weight of a Family of 4 is 57.5 kg. If the second highest weighing member is not considered, the average drops by 0.5 kg. What is the weight of the heaviest member if it is 16 kg more than the second highest weighing member?
questions.explanation: Weight of the second highest weighing member = 57.5*4 - 57*3 = 59 kg Weight of the heaviest member = 59 + 16 = 75 kg

questions.question: Polka borrowed a sum of Rs. 490200 from a bank at 7% simple interest scheme, payable in five equal annual installments. Find the annual installment amount to be paid by him, each year?
questions.explanation: Principal = Rs. 490200 Amount to be paid back (along with interest) = Rs. (490200 X 1.35) = Rs. 661770. Let each annual installment amount paid back by Polka be Rs. X. For the first installment, interest will be applicable for 4 years. Hence effective amount paid back = Rs. 1.28X For the second installment, interest will be applicable for 3 years. Hence effective amount paid back = Rs. 1.21X For the third installment, interest will be applicable for 2 years. Hence effective amount paid back = Rs. 1.14X For the fourth installment, interest will be applicable for 1 year. Hence effective amount paid back = Rs. 1.07X For the fifth installment, interest will be applicable for 0 years. Hence effective amount paid back = Rs. X Total amount paid back effectively = Rs. (1.28 + 1.21 + 1.14 + 1.07 + 1)X = Rs. 661770 or 5.7X = 661770 or X = Rs. 116100.

questions.question: 2 men and 7 boys together can complete a piece of work in 14 days, while 3 men and 8 boys together can do it in 11 days. Then in how many days will 8 men and 6 boys do thrice that work?
questions.explanation: 2 men and 7 boys do in 1 day = 1/14........(1) 3 men and 8 boys do in one day = 1/11.......(2) Multiplying equation (1) by 3 6 men and 21 boys do in 1 day =3/14 work......(3) Multiplying equation (2) by 2 6 men and 16 boys do in one day = 2/11......(4) Subtracting equation (3) by equation (4) 5 boys will do in one day = 5/154 work 1 boys will do in one day = 1/154 work And 1 man will do in 1 day =1/77 work 8 men and 6 boys will do in 1 day = 8/77 + 6/154 = ⅐ work 8 men and 6 boys will do unit work in = 7 days 8 men and 6 boys will do thrice work in7x3 = 21 days.

questions.question: Solve the given expression - CotA * (1 - 4Sin²A)/(3 - 4Sin²A)
questions.explanation: CotA * (1 - 4Sin²A)/(3 - 4Sin²A) => CosA/SinA * (1 - 4(1 - Cos²A))/(3 - 4Sin²A)) => CosA/SinA * (1 - 4 + 4Cos²A)/(3 - 4Sin²A) => CosA/SinA * (4Cos²A - 3)/(3 - 4Sin²A) => (4Cos³A - 3CosA)/(3SinA - 4Sin³A) => Cos3A/Sin3A => Cot3A

questions.question: Find the value of ½ * [{1 + Tan²(45⁰ + A)}/Tan(45⁰ + A)].
questions.explanation: ½ * [{1 + Tan²(45⁰ + A)}/Tan(45⁰ + A)] [1 + Tan²(45⁰ + A)]/2Tan(45⁰ + A) (Since, Sin2A = 2TanA/(1 + Tan²A)) => 1/Sin2(45⁰ + A) = 1/Sin(90⁰ + 2A) => 1/Cos2A .. (Since, Sin(90⁰ + θ) = Cosθ) = Sec2A

questions.question: Aloknath booked a banquet hall for his daughter's marriage, the dimensions of which are 40 feet by 32 feet. He spent ₹57600 for carpeting the floor. What is the cost of carpeting per square feet?
questions.explanation: Area of the hall = 40 * 32 = 1280 feet² Cost of carpeting per square foot = 57600/1280 = ₹45

questions.question: Ali Zafar, the butcher sells stale meat at 20% loss on his cost price. Although he uses inaccurate weights which weigh 25% less. What is his percentage profit or loss?
questions.explanation: Let the cost price of 1 kg of meat be ₹1000. Selling price = 1000 - 20% of 1000 = ₹800. But he sells 25% less in weight i.e. 750 g instead of 1000 g. => Selling price of 750 g of meat = ₹800 But the cost price of 750 g of meat = ₹750 Actual gain = 800 - 750 = ₹50 % gain = 50/750 * 100 = 6.67%

questions.question: In the following diagram XY is the diameter of the circle with centre O. What is the value of 'x'? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3002/image010.png)
questions.explanation: Angle inscribed inside the semicircle is right angle. So, m(∠XZY) = 90⁰ m(∠XZW) = 90⁰................(Adjacent angles) In triangle WXZ (x +2x) = 90⁰ (x) = 30⁰

questions.question: Find the value of ∠MNO in the given figure, if ∠MPO = 50⁰ and ∠NMP = 80⁰. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p2943/image004.png)
questions.explanation: MNOP is a quadrilateral. Let ∠MNO = x⁰ ∠MPO = 50⁰, ∠NMP = 80⁰ Interior ∠NOP of quadrilateral MNOP = 360⁰ - (2 * ∠NMP) = 360⁰ - 2 * 80⁰ = 200⁰ (The angle subtended by an arc at the centre is double the angle subtended by it at any point on the remaining part of the circle.) Now ∠MNO + ∠MPO + ∠NOP + ∠NMP = 360⁰ => x⁰ + 50⁰ + 200⁰ + 80⁰ = 360⁰ => x = 30⁰

questions.question: A car travelling with a speed of 60 km/hr catches another car travelling in the same direction and then leaves it 120 m behind in 18 seconds. What is the speed of the second car?
questions.explanation: 60 km/hr = 50/3 m/s Let the speed of the second car be x m/s. The relative speed between the two cars = (50/3 - x) m/s Now, 50/3 - x = 120/18 => x = 10 m/s = 36 km/hr

questions.question: A triangle ABC is right angled at A and AD is perpendicular to BC. AD = 5 cm and BD = 4 cm. Find the length of BC (in cm).
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1006/image006.jpg) AD² + DB² = AB² 25 + 16 = AB² AB² = 41 => AB = √41 Also, triangle ABC ~ triangle DBA AB/DB = BC/AB AB² = BC*DB 41 = BC*4 BC = 41/4 cm

questions.question: A number is divided successively in order by 4, 5 and 7. The remainders were respectively 3, 4 and 2. The number is
questions.explanation: Starting from last, 7 x 1 + 2 = 9 5 x 9 + 4 = 49 4 x 49 + 3 = 199 Therefore, required number is 199.

questions.question: When the numerator is doubled and 7 is added to the denominator the fraction becomes ⅔ and when 3 is subtracted from both the numerator and the denominator the fraction becomes ½, what is the fraction?
questions.explanation: Let the fraction be x/y So, 2x/(y + 7) = ⅔ => 2y + 14 = 6x => y = 3x - 7 Also, (x - 3)/(y - 3) = ½ => y - 3 = 2x - 6 => 3x - 7 - 3 = 2x - 6 => x = 4 ∴ y = 3*4 - 7 = 5 So, the fraction is ⅘

questions.question: What is the unit digit of (23 x 3 + 15 x 7)⁴⁸?
questions.explanation: The concept of cyclicity is used to identify the last digit of the number. Cyclicity chart: NumberCyclicityUnit digit values Power 1Power 2Power 3Power 4 010 111 242486 343971 4246 515 616 747931 848426 9291 (Note: remainder = power. When remainder is 0, power = cyclicity) = (23 x 3 + 15 x 7)⁴⁸ = (69 + 105)⁴⁸ = 174⁴⁸ In 174⁴⁸: Unit digit of 174 is 4. 4⁴⁸: From the cyclicity chart, cyclicity of 4 is 2. 48/2 remainder = 0 =power 2 Unit digit of 174⁴⁸: 6
